stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Tomás Saraceno is an Argentine artist who uses suspended materials as his primary medium of expression.
  • He created his work Cloud Cities in 2011.
  • Cloud Cities consisted of interconnected transparent spheres suspended high above the gallery floor.
  • Visitors could climb into the spheres and walk among them on a network of cables.
stem: The student wants to describe Cloud Cities to an audience unfamiliar with Tomás Saraceno. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Created in 2011, Cloud Cities—a work by Argentine artist Tomás Saraceno that uses suspended materials—consisted of interconnected transparent spheres suspended high above the gallery floor.
The work consisted of interconnected transparent spheres suspended high above the gallery floor, with visitors able to climb into the spheres.
Tomás Saraceno, an Argentine artist who uses suspended materials, created Cloud Cities in 2011.
Visitors to Cloud Cities could climb into the spheres and walk among them on a network of cables.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence describes Cloud Cities for an audience unfamiliar with Saraceno, mentioning the interconnected transparent spheres suspended high above the gallery floor while identifying Saraceno as an Argentine artist who uses suspended materials.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence describes the work but doesn’t identify Saraceno for the unfamiliar audience. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence identifies Saraceno but doesn’t describe Cloud Cities. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence notes how visitors interacted with the work but doesn’t identify Saraceno.
